content:
Manganese is a critical material in laser cleaning applications, often encountered as a surface contaminant or alloy component in industrial settings like steel processing. Its unique properties influence cleaning efficacy, especially under high-energy laser pulses. Below is a detailed table of material properties for Manganese relevant to laser cleaning, ensuring optimal parameter selection for residue removal without substrate damage. Note that Manganese oxides can form tenacious layers; laser cleaning must balance ablation thresholds to avoid surface pitting—something I’ve seen in high-carbon steel applications.

| Property                  | Value                          |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Atomic Number             | 25                            |
| Density                   | 7.21 g/cm³                    |
| Melting Point             | 1246°C                        |
| Thermal Conductivity      | 7.81 W/(m·K)                  |
| Specific Heat Capacity    | 0.479 J/(g·K)                 |
| Reflectivity (at 1064 nm) | ~60% (typical Nd:YAG laser wavelength)
